advanced.title: Auto-pick weights
advanced.description: How the auto-pick understands your watch habits. Recent rows count more than old ones; total minutes watched matters more than total episodes.

advanced.lockInactiveDescription: Total weighted history {current} is below the floor {floor}. Watch a bit more and the auto-pick will start learning.
advanced.coarseDescription: Drives the lock decision (which language and dub-vs-sub).
advanced.fineDescription: Picks the team within the lock. Showing top 8 by weight.
advanced.resetDescription: Wipes per-anime locks. Your watch history is kept, so the auto-pick will relearn from scratch.
advanced.resetConfirm: Reset all per-anime preferences? Your watch history will be kept, but the auto-pick will start fresh.
advanced.resetSuccess: Preferences reset. The next anime you open will use community popularity until weights rebuild.

cert.instructionsWindows: Windows: open the downloaded .p12, choose “Current user”, enter the password
cert.instructionsMacos: macOS: open the .p12, enter the password — the certificate goes into your Keychain
cert.instructionsIos: iOS: download the .p12, then Settings → General → VPN & Device Management → install the profile
cert.instructionsAndroid: Android: Settings → Security → Encryption & credentials → Install a certificate (VPN & apps)
cert.instructionsLinux: Linux: import the .p12 into your browser (Settings → Privacy → Certificates)
cert.instructionsFirefox: Firefox (any OS): doesn't see system-store certificates — import the .p12 into Firefox itself: Settings → Privacy & Security → Certificates → View Certificates → Your Certificates → Import. There, also pick “Select one automatically” — Firefox stops asking for the certificate (otherwise a private window may ask twice: it opens two parallel connections)

recs.title: Recs Debug
recs.pickerHelp: Enter a username, public ID, or UUID to view that user's rec breakdown.
recs.tableCaption: Recommendation candidates for user, ranked by final score
recs.s1Desc: Predicted score via Pearson-correlated taste neighbours (k-NN over the user's anime_list scores). Cold-start: needs enough rated anime, otherwise contributes 0.
recs.s2Desc: Max Jaccard overlap between the candidate's genres and the user's top-rated seed anime.
recs.s3Desc: Distinct users who started watching the anime in the last 30 days (population trending).
recs.s4Desc: Pure metadata freshness: ongoing, or aired within the last 90 days.
recs.s5Desc: Time-weighted TF-IDF affinity over tags (0.30), studios (0.25), genres (0.15), demographic (0.10), source (0.10), kind (0.10) from watch history.
recs.s6Desc: Post-rank pin at #1: "Because you finished X". Cascade: local co-occurrence → Shikimori /similar → score-5 fallback. Fires on a completed anime scored ≥7.
recs.s7Desc: Negative signal: max genre+tag Jaccard vs anime the user DROPPED (drops scored ≥7 excluded; needs ≥2 dropped seeds). Negative weight demotes look-alikes.
recs.s11Desc: Not a score — removes anime already on the user's list (completed / dropped / hidden) from the candidate pool. See the filter audit panel below.
recs.s12Desc: Post-rank greedy MMR re-rank (λ=0.3, similarity over genres+studios, max 3 items with an identical genre set). The arrow in the Rank column shows how far S12 moved a row.

userResolve.placeholder: username, public_id, Telegram ID, or UUID

providers.title: Scraper providers
providers.intro: Turn each provider's probing on (Auto) or off (Disabled). While Auto, the machine moves it in and out of the auto-failover chain by health on its own — down providers are parked to hand-selectable, healthy ones rejoin automatically.
providers.probeToggleLabel: Set {name} probe status: Auto or Disabled
providers.confirmDisableBody: Disabling removes {name} from playback entirely — it won't be probed or hand-selectable. (A down provider is already auto-parked out of the failover chain without disabling.)

auto.success: {name} probing enabled — the machine now manages its failover state

providersPlaceholder.title: No providers
providersPlaceholder.description: No scraper providers are registered yet.

maintenance.title: Maintenance routines
maintenance.intro: Pause or resume background routines and tune their safe knobs. Pausing a host routine skips its next run — the schedule stays intact.
maintenance.confirmPauseBody: {name} will skip its runs until you resume it. Auto-recovery and cleanup won't happen while paused.

q1.a: AnimeEnigma is a self-hosted anime streaming platform with Shikimori and MAL integration. It combines metadata from Shikimori with video from multiple sources, plus tools like recommendations, OP/ED ratings, watch parties, and personal lists — all in one place.

q2.q: Is it free? Are there ads?
q2.a: Yes, free and ad-free. The platform is self-hosted by a small group, not a commercial service. There's no advertising, no tracking pixels, and no subscription tier — everyone gets the same access.

q3.q: Where do the videos come from?
q3.a: AnimeEnigma combines several sources in one player, including Kodik for Russian tracks, English-language providers, and first-party library content. Available sources are selected from the live provider feed, and supported streams are proxied so your IP does not contact the external CDN directly.

q4.a: We combine five signals: top-list similarity (S1), genre affinity (S2), global trending (S3), rating boost (S4), and seasonal freshness (S5). Each signal contributes a weighted score and the final list is mixed. No personal data leaves the server — recommendations are computed locally from your activity.

q5.q: Can I import my MAL list?
q5.a: Yes. Go to Profile → Settings → MAL Import and enter your MAL username. The importer pulls your full list (statuses, scores, episodes watched, dates) and resolves each title to the AnimeEnigma catalog. Re-running the import updates entries that changed on MAL.

q6.a: Not yet — the web app is fully mobile-responsive and works offline-ish via the browser cache. A native app may come later, but for now installing the site as a PWA (Add to Home Screen) is the closest thing.

q7.q: How do I report a bug or request a feature?
q7.a: Use the "Send feedback" link in the page footer — pick Bug, Issue, or Feature, optionally describe it, and submit. The report is saved on the server and an admin gets a Telegram notification. Console logs and network requests are auto-attached to help debugging.

q8.a: A small self-hosted group of anime fans. The code is open and contributions are welcome via the project repository. No company, no shareholders, no roadmap pressure — just folks who wanted a better way to watch anime.

home.title: Home starts here
home.body: The logo always takes you home — to continue watching, recommendations, and fresh releases.

search.title: Search your way
search.body: Enter a title in any language — suggestions appear as you type, and Enter opens all results.

catalog.title: The full catalog, with filters
catalog.body: Filter anime by genre, year, status, and type, then sort the results.

schedule.title: Never miss a new episode
schedule.body: The schedule shows releases by day and respects your time zone.

account.title: Your list and progress stay close
account.body: Your profile keeps your watchlist, scores, and progress. On mobile, find these sections in the menu.

feedback.title: Tell us when something is off
feedback.body: Use this button to send a bug, question, or idea. For playback issues, include the anime, episode, and selected source.

screen.title: The main player screen
screen.body: Click or tap to pause, and double-tap on a phone to seek by 10 seconds. Your viewing position is saved automatically.

episodes.title: Episodes and progress
episodes.body: Choose episodes here and see saved progress and watched marks. In the installed app, this is also where you can download a season.

source.title: Sources, audio, and language
source.body: The source panel combines DUB and RAW, languages, and translation teams. If a stream breaks, the player automatically tries the next suitable source.

subtitles.title: Subtitles under control
subtitles.body: The CC button selects a subtitle language and opens extended search. You can also adjust size, background, timing, and speech auto-sync.

settings.title: Quality and viewing pace
settings.body: Settings include quality, speed, automatic next episode, intro and outro skipping, and a link to the current moment.

view.title: Theater or fullscreen
view.body: On desktop, theater mode widens the player without hiding the page. Fullscreen works everywhere, while PiP keeps video above other windows.

zundamon.title: Zundamon Voice Lab
zundamon.subtitle: The actual Zundamon voice, synthesized by our dedicated VOICEVOX engine.
zundamon.imageAlt: Zundamon smiling beside a studio microphone
zundamon.portraitCaption: Ready for one more lineなのだ!
zundamon.studioHint: The dedicated server uses the official VOICEVOX Zundamon model. It will never substitute a generic browser voice.
zundamon.engineUnavailableBody: The voice engine is not responding right now. Please retry shortly.
zundamon.speakerMissingBody: The engine responded, but the official ずんだもん model is unavailable.
zundamon.degradedBody: VOICEVOX is a low-priority service and pauses automatically while the platform is under load.
zundamon.sample: こんにちはなのだ！今日はどんなアニメを見るのだ？
zundamon.statusDegraded: Voice synthesis is paused while the platform is under load.
zundamon.statusBusy: Zundamon is already speaking. Try again in a moment.
zundamon.statusError: VOICEVOX could not create or play this line.
zundamon.serverBody: Text is sent to AnimeEnigma's private VOICEVOX container only to create this audio. The engine is automatically shed under load and audio is not cached.

tips.title: Secret tips & hotkeys
tips.subtitle: You found the hidden page. Everything the interface never tells you — keyboard shortcuts and features tucked away around the site.

keys.homeEnd: To the start / end (progress bar focused)
keys.nextEpisodeCard: Next episode (while the Up-Next prompt is up)
keys.help: Open this secret page (works almost anywhere)
keys.escape: Close modals, drawers, the poster lightbox, theater mode

roulette.title: Secret feature roulette
roulette.body: The «Secret feature» button in the footer teleports you to a random hidden page — this one included.

rawdub.title: RAW vs DUB
rawdub.body: The switch at the top of the player: DUB is localized voice-over, RAW is the original Japanese audio — pair it with subtitles from the menu.

autosync.title: Subtitle auto-sync
autosync.body: Subtitles drifting? The subtitles menu has an auto-sync switch that aligns timing to detected speech automatically.

storyboard.title: Timeline previews
storyboard.body: Hover the player timeline to peek at scene thumbnails before you seek.

contextmenu.title: Poster context menu
contextmenu.body: Right-click any anime poster: open in a new tab, mark the next episode watched, or download a whole season.

theater.title: Theater mode
theater.body: The button in the player controls stretches video across the page width — halfway between windowed and fullscreen.

pwa.title: Install as an app
pwa.body: Install AnimeEnigma as an app (PWA) to unlock offline episode downloads — with a Wi-Fi-only option.

watchtogether.title: Watch together
watchtogether.body: The button inside the player creates a private room; playback stays in sync for everyone, chat included.

report.title: Report a broken stream
report.body: Stream won't play? The report button in the player pings the admins directly — with logs attached.

tips.secretNote: This page isn't in any menu. Now you know why. Keep it secret… or don't.
